assumptions: The developer does not have a BrowserStack account, plan, Username, or Access key.
For the representative route, Node.js, Git, the BrowserStack Cypress CLI, sample repository, and configuration are not assumed.

developer_goal: Choose one BrowserStack product and first result; the representative route runs the Cypress Kitchen Sink sample against its public URL and inspects the cloud result.

first_success.outcome: Obtain BrowserStack credentials, install the Cypress CLI, clone and configure the Kitchen Sink sample for the public app, execute it on BrowserStack, and inspect its cloud build and logs.
first_success.observable_signal: The CLI reports completed test statuses, the results folder contains HTML and JSON build summaries, and Automate groups the run by browser combination with a detailed view containing video and text logs.

route_selection.surface: Automate: run your first Cypress test (Kitchen Sink) on a public URL
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Automate: run your first Cypress test (Kitchen Sink) on a public URL
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Automate with Selenium
candidates.reason: BrowserStack labels Selenium widely used, but the path further branches by language, framework, build tool, sample versus existing suite, and local versus public app.

candidates.name: Automate with Playwright
candidates.reason: A peer browser-automation framework with different SDK and runner setup.

candidates.name: App Automate
candidates.reason: Mobile automation requires Appium, Espresso, XCUITest, Flutter, Detox, Maestro, or Smart TV plus an app artifact or URL.

required_path.action: Complete BrowserStack account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new BrowserStack account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected BrowserStack candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The CLI reports completed test statuses, the results folder contains HTML and JSON build summaries, and Automate groups the run by browser combination with a detailed view containing video and text logs.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'A BrowserStack Free Account or purchased plan with access to Automate. The current signup page offers Google sign-in or a full-name, business-email, and password route and requires acceptance of the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy. The signup page advertises 100 automated browser-testing minutes, while the current free-trial FAQ states 60 Automat
{'order': 2, 'type': 'access', 'requirement': 'BrowserStack Username and Access key from account settings.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}
{'order': 3, 'type': 'knowledge', 'requirement': 'Basic Cypress fundamentals for the representative route.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}

external_gates.description: BrowserStack has many products with unrelated first results.
Automate requires a framework, language, and sample-versus-existing-suite choice.
The guide requires a Free Account or purchased plan with Automate access, and signup requires Google identity or full name, business email, and password.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: In the selected Run your first Cypress test route, first success is demonstrated when Automate shows the completed run grouped by browser with a detailed view containing video and text logs.

excluded.item: Integrating the developer's existing test suite.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Running tests in parallel beyond the sample configuration and optimizing concurrency.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Testing a private or local application when the public branch is selected.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Advanced capabilities, geolocation, network conditions, browsers and devices, CI integration, debugging, analytics, AI agents, and production secret management.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

uncertainties.question: Which BrowserStack product and test result should represent the platform?
uncertainties.impact: A manual session, automated browser build, mobile app session, accessibility scan, visual diff, low-code recording, load test, or report has different prerequisites and success.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The company and docs expose many products, and the roster supplies no test intent.

uncertainties.question: Which Automate framework, language, browser matrix, and application target applies?
uncertainties.impact: These choices change dependencies, credentials placement, sample code, commands, scheduling, and result evidence.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The official overview requires framework and language selection; Cypress and public Kitchen Sink are representative only.

uncertainties.question: Which official free automated-browser allowance applies to a newly created account?
uncertainties.impact: A 60-minute rather than 100-minute allowance changes how much of a sample matrix can complete before quota exhaustion.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The current signup page advertises 100 automated browser-testing minutes, while the current free-trial FAQ lists 60 Automate minutes.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does BrowserStack show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
